steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Two statements must each be tested: does removing 76.2 degreesF (from the high-end bin [75, 80)) necessarily lower the mean? The median? Whichever statements are always true determine the answer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Statement I — mean decreases: The mean is pulled up by the two outlier values near 76-80 degreesF while the other 9 values cluster in [40, 60). Removing a value that is well above the mean always lowers the mean. Statement I must be true. ✓
  • Statement II — median decreases: With 11 values the median is the 6th ordered value, which falls in [50, 55). After removing 76.2 degreesF there are 10 values, and the new median is the average of the 5th and 6th values. Both the 5th and 6th values still fall in [50, 55), so the median stays in that same range — it does not necessarily decrease. Statement II is not guaranteed. ✗
  • "I only": Statement I is always true; Statement II is not. ✓
  • "II only": Statement II is not guaranteed. ✗
  • "I and II": Statement II fails. ✗
  • "Neither I nor II": Statement I is always true. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
